strands.name: Number Sense
strands.description: Subitizing, counting, ten-frames, place value, comparing — building a feel for how numbers live.

strands.name: Add & Subtract Structures
strands.description: Part-whole, change, comparison — the *shapes* of arithmetic, not just the answers.

strands.name: Mystery Numbers
strands.description: Secret-number puzzles that plant the seed of algebra without symbols.

strands.name: Equality & Balance
strands.description: Equals means *the same as*. True/false equations, balance scales, both-sides thinking.

strands.name: Patterns
strands.description: Repeating, growing, function-machine patterns. Predicting and explaining.

strands.name: Logic & Sorting
strands.description: Attribute games, 'is not' clues, ordering puzzles. Reasoning under constraints.

strands.name: Geometry & Space
strands.description: Shapes, symmetry, paper folding, mental rotation, tangram, position words.

strands.name: Measurement
strands.description: Length, time, money, weight — using both standard and child-invented units.

strands.name: Counting Ways
strands.description: How many ways? Outfits, paths, breakdowns of a number — early combinatorics.

strands.name: Math Games
strands.description: Two-player strategy games (Nim, Race-to-100, Pig). Strategy = invariant thinking.

problems.title: Quick look: 1 dots
problems.prompt: I'll flash 1 dots. Tell me how many — without counting one-by-one. How did you 'see' it?
hints: Picture the dots in your mind after they disappear.
Did you see 1 as a whole, or as smaller groups?
Two and -1 make 1. Three and -2 make 1.
problems.explain_prompt: What did your eyes 'grab' first?

problems.title: Quick look: 2 dots
problems.prompt: I'll flash 2 dots. Tell me how many — without counting one-by-one. How did you 'see' it?
hints: Picture the dots in your mind after they disappear.
Did you see 2 as a whole, or as smaller groups?
Two and 0 make 2. Three and -1 make 2.
problems.explain_prompt: What did your eyes 'grab' first?

problems.title: Quick look: 3 dots
problems.prompt: I'll flash 3 dots. Tell me how many — without counting one-by-one. How did you 'see' it?
hints: Picture the dots in your mind after they disappear.
Did you see 3 as a whole, or as smaller groups?
Two and 1 make 3. Three and 0 make 3.
problems.explain_prompt: What did your eyes 'grab' first?

problems.title: Quick look: 4 dots
problems.prompt: I'll flash 4 dots. Tell me how many — without counting one-by-one. How did you 'see' it?
hints: Picture the dots in your mind after they disappear.
Did you see 4 as a whole, or as smaller groups?
Two and 2 make 4. Three and 1 make 4.
problems.explain_prompt: What did your eyes 'grab' first?

problems.title: Quick look: 5 dots
problems.prompt: I'll flash 5 dots. Tell me how many — without counting one-by-one. How did you 'see' it?
hints: Picture the dots in your mind after they disappear.
Did you see 5 as a whole, or as smaller groups?
Two and 3 make 5. Three and 2 make 5.
problems.explain_prompt: What did your eyes 'grab' first?

problems.title: Quick look: 6 dots
problems.prompt: I'll flash 6 dots. Tell me how many — without counting one-by-one. How did you 'see' it?
hints: Picture the dots in your mind after they disappear.
Did you see 6 as a whole, or as smaller groups?
Two and 4 make 6. Three and 3 make 6.
problems.explain_prompt: What did your eyes 'grab' first?

problems.title: Counting up the stairs
problems.prompt: Count from 1 up to 15 like climbing stairs. Now count back down. Where does it feel hardest?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Skip count by 2s
problems.prompt: Count by 2s up to 20: 2, 4, 6 ... How would you say it as 'pairs of socks'?
problems.parent_extension: Lay out 10 pennies in pairs. Skip-count to 20 by touching pairs.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Skip count by 5s
problems.prompt: Count by 5s to 50. Then to 100. What pattern do the last digits make?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Skip count by 10s
problems.prompt: Count by 10s to 100. Now start at 7 and add 10 each time: 7, 17, 27 ... what do you notice?
hints: Each jump = 1 in the tens place.
The ones digit stays the same.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Ten-frame for 7
problems.prompt: Fill a ten-frame to show 7. How many empty squares? What does that tell you?
hints: Count the empty boxes to see how far from 10.
problems.explain_prompt: How does the ten-frame help you see 'how many to 10'?

problems.title: Two ways to see 9
problems.prompt: Show 9 on a ten-frame two different ways. Are they really different — or the same?
hints: Try 5 in the top row and 4 below.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Bigger: 23 or 32?
problems.prompt: Which is bigger, 23 or 32? Show with tens-and-ones blocks. How many tens does each one have?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Build 47
problems.prompt: Build 47 with tens and ones blocks. How many tens? How many ones? Now make it with different blocks but the same value.
hints: Could you use 3 tens and 17 ones?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Even or odd: 12 cookies
problems.prompt: You have 12 cookies. Can you split them into 2 equal piles? What about 13 cookies? Why does one work and one doesn't?
problems.explain_prompt: What is special about every odd number?
problems.parent_extension: Look at house numbers on a walk. Predict odd or even before counting.

problems.title: The animal race
problems.prompt: A turtle, rabbit, fox, and bear race. The fox is third. The rabbit is right after the fox. Who is fourth? Where is the turtle?
hints: Draw four spots in a line.
Put the fox in the third spot.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Hundred-chart mystery: 37
problems.prompt: On a hundred chart, what number is right above 37? Right below? To the left? To the right? What pattern do you see?
problems.explain_prompt: Why does going up give 10 less?

problems.title: Double 7
problems.prompt: What is 7 + 7? How can you use it to find 7 + 8?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Friends of ten
problems.prompt: Two numbers add to 10. One of them is 8. What is the other? Now what if one is 3?
hints: Use your fingers — show 8, count up to 10.
Ten-frame: empty boxes show the partner.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: One more, one less
problems.prompt: What's one more than 9? One less than 9? What's one more than 19? One less than 20?
hints: Climb up one stair / down one stair.
Tens are speed bumps — pause and notice.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: All the ways to make 10
problems.prompt: Find all the ways to make 10 using two whole numbers (zero is okay). How do you know you found them all?
problems.explain_prompt: Why can you stop at 5+5?
problems.parent_extension: Try 'all the ways to make 7' on the kitchen table with cereal pieces.

problems.title: Three numbers that make 7
problems.prompt: Find three different whole numbers that add to 7. How many sets can you find? (Order doesn't count.)
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Add by tens: 30 + 40
problems.prompt: What is 30 + 40? How is it like 3 + 4? What is 35 + 40?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Red and blue blocks
problems.prompt: There are red and blue blocks. I made 8 blocks total. Show me three different ways the reds and blues could split.
problems.explain_prompt: How are these all 'the same' (8 total) and 'different' (different splits)?
problems.parent_extension: With 10 grapes, ask: how many can you eat now if you save some for later?

problems.title: Frogs on the log
problems.prompt: 3 frogs sat on a log. 4 more hopped on. How many frogs now? Tell the story with your fingers.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Cookie monster
problems.prompt: There were 9 cookies on a plate. The cookie monster ate 4. How many cookies are left?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Sticker comparison
problems.prompt: Lila has 6 stickers. Sam has 9 stickers. How many more stickers does Sam have than Lila?
hints: Line them up — Lila's row, Sam's row.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Missing addend
problems.prompt: 7 children are at the park. Some more arrive, and now there are 13. How many more came?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Flip the comparison
problems.prompt: Lila has 4 fewer marbles than Sam. Sam has 11. How many does Lila have? Now: if Lila has 7 and that's 4 fewer than Sam, how many does Sam have?
problems.explain_prompt: The story changed direction. What stayed the same?

problems.title: The bus problem
problems.prompt: 8 kids were on the bus. At the next stop, 5 got off and 3 got on. How many kids on the bus now?
hints: What happens first? People get off.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Buying toys
problems.prompt: Maya had 10 dollars. She bought a toy for 4 dollars and got 2 dollars from her brother. How much money does she have now?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Carrots in two baskets
problems.prompt: A rabbit has 10 carrots in two baskets. The first basket has 4. How many in the second basket?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Three baskets
problems.prompt: Three baskets hold 15 apples in all. The first has 6 apples; the second has 4. How many in the third?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Ages
problems.prompt: Anna is 6. Her brother is 9. How much older is the brother? In two years, what will the difference be?
hints: Differences stay the same as both grow!
problems.explain_prompt: Why doesn't the difference change as time passes?
problems.parent_extension: Ask: in 10 years, how much older will the brother be?

problems.title: Near doubles: 6 + 7
problems.prompt: What is 6 + 7? Use 6 + 6 to help you.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Bridge through 10
problems.prompt: What is 8 + 5? Try splitting the 5 into 2 + 3 to get to 10 first.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: 2 and 3
problems.prompt: There are 2 ducks in the pond. 3 more swim over. How many ducks now?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: 4 and 5
problems.prompt: There are 4 ducks in the pond. 5 more swim over. How many ducks now?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: 6 and 4
problems.prompt: There are 6 ducks in the pond. 4 more swim over. How many ducks now?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: 5 and 7
problems.prompt: There are 5 ducks in the pond. 7 more swim over. How many ducks now?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: 8 and 6
problems.prompt: There are 8 ducks in the pond. 6 more swim over. How many ducks now?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: 9 and 4
problems.prompt: There are 9 ducks in the pond. 4 more swim over. How many ducks now?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Secret number (+4 = 9)
problems.prompt: I'm thinking of a secret number. If I add 4, I get 9. What is my number?
problems.explain_prompt: How is undoing 'add 4' the same as 'subtract 4'?

problems.title: Secret number (doubled = 12)
problems.prompt: If I double my secret number, I get 12. What is the number?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Secret number (-3 = 7)
problems.prompt: A secret number minus 3 is 7. What is it?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Two clues
problems.prompt: My number is between 10 and 20. It's odd. The digits add to 7. What is it?
hints: Wait — 13's digits are 1 and 3, that's 4. Try 16... not odd. Re-check 13... 1+3=4, not 7. Try 25? Out of range. Hmm — re-read.
problems.explain_prompt: Which clue narrowed it the most?

problems.title: Three-clue mystery
problems.prompt: My number is even. It's between 20 and 40. The digits add to 6. What is it?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Add then subtract
problems.prompt: A secret number plus 5, then minus 2, gives 10. What was the number?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Box on the balance
problems.prompt: A balance scale shows: a box plus 3 marbles balances 8 marbles. How many marbles in the box?
problems.explain_prompt: Why does taking 3 from both sides keep it balanced?

problems.title: Two boxes balance
problems.prompt: Two equal boxes plus 2 marbles balance 12 marbles. How many marbles per box?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Number plus itself
problems.prompt: A number plus itself plus 1 is 11. What is the number?
hints: Now find a doubles fact for 10.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Hidden fingers
problems.prompt: I'm holding up some fingers behind my back. Together with the 3 in front, I'm showing 8 fingers total. How many are hidden?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Hidden card
problems.prompt: Two cards are face-down. I can see a 4 and the total is 11. What is the hidden card?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Sticker pages
problems.prompt: A sticker book has 4 pages with stickers and 1 hidden page. Total stickers = 18. The four visible pages have 3, 5, 4, 4. How many on the hidden page?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: True or false: 3 + 4 = 7
problems.prompt: Is 3 + 4 = 7 a true statement? How do you know?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: True or false: 5 + 2 = 3 + 4
problems.prompt: Is 5 + 2 = 3 + 4 true or false? Why? Equals doesn't mean 'the answer is' — it means 'is the same as'.
problems.explain_prompt: What does '=' really mean to you?

problems.title: Fill the blank: __ + 3 = 7
problems.prompt: What number goes in the blank? __ + 3 = 7
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Fill the blank: 12 = __ + 7
problems.prompt: What number makes 12 = __ + 7 true?
problems.explain_prompt: Why is the equals sign in the middle still working?

problems.title: Balance: blocks
problems.prompt: One side of the scale has 3 blocks. The other side has 6 blocks. How many do you add to the lighter side to balance?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Two ways to make the same value
problems.prompt: Write two different addition expressions that both equal 10. Now write one with subtraction. Connect them with =.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Both sides equal: 7 + 3 = __ + 5
problems.prompt: What number goes in the blank? 7 + 3 = __ + 5. Don't 'do' the left first — think about what makes both sides match.
problems.explain_prompt: What goes wrong if you write 7 + 3 = 10 + 5?

problems.prompt: What number makes 9 - 2 = __ - 5 true?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Two boxes balance
problems.prompt: A balance shows: 2 boxes balance 10 marbles. Each box has the same number. How many marbles in one box?
hints: Two equal boxes share the load.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Box + 4 = 11
problems.prompt: One box plus 4 marbles balances 11 marbles. How many marbles in the box? Show with a balance picture.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: True or false: 8 = 8
problems.prompt: Is 8 = 8 a true equation? What about 8 = 5 + 3? What about 5 + 3 = 8?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Triangle, square, triangle ...
problems.prompt: A pattern: △ ◻ △ ◻ △ ◻ ___ ___ ___. What comes next? What about the 10th shape?
problems.explain_prompt: What's the smallest piece that repeats?

problems.title: Triangle, square, square ...
problems.prompt: Pattern: △ ◻ ◻ △ ◻ ◻ △ ___ ___. What comes next?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Three-color pattern
problems.prompt: 🔴🔵🟢🔴🔵🟢🔴 ___ ___. What comes next?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Growing pattern
problems.prompt: 1, 3, 5, 7 ... what's next? What's the 10th number?
problems.explain_prompt: How would you describe the rule in words?

problems.title: Doubling pattern
problems.prompt: 2, 4, 8, 16 ... what's next? What's the rule?
hints: Each one is twice the one before.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Function machine: + 3
problems.prompt: A machine: input 1 → output 4. Input 2 → 5. Input 5 → 8. What does it do? If input is 9, what's output?
hints: Each output is 3 more than input.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Function machine: double
problems.prompt: Machine: 3 → 6, 5 → 10, 7 → 14. What does it do? If 11 goes in, what comes out? What goes in to make 20 come out?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Staircase of squares
problems.prompt: Build a staircase: row 1 has 1 square, row 2 has 2, row 3 has 3, row 4 has 4. How many squares total in 4 rows? How about 5 rows?
problems.explain_prompt: Notice anything that pairs up to make 10s?

problems.title: Find the mistake
problems.prompt: A pattern goes 🔴 🔴 🔵 🔴 🔴 🔵 🔴 🔵 🔴 🔴 🔵. One thing is out of place. Which?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Patterns on the hundred chart
problems.prompt: On a hundred chart, color all the numbers ending in 5. What does it look like? Now numbers ending in 0. What patterns do you see together?
problems.explain_prompt: Why do they form straight lines?

problems.title: Three animals in line
problems.prompt: A fox, rabbit, and bear are in a line. The fox is not first. The bear is right after the rabbit. Find an order.
hints: Try the bear first — does it work?
Bear after rabbit means rabbit before bear.
Fox not first → fox is 2nd or 3rd.
problems.explain_prompt: Which clue ruled out the most options?

problems.title: Four kids in line
problems.prompt: Anna, Ben, Coco, Dev are in a line. Anna is right behind Ben. Coco is first. Dev is last. What order are they in?
hints: Place the fixed people: Coco 1st, Dev 4th.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Sort the buttons
problems.prompt: You have buttons: red round, red square, blue round, blue square, red triangle. Find a way to sort them into 2 groups, then find a different way.
hints: What attributes do the buttons have?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Is not / is
problems.prompt: I'm thinking of a fruit. It is NOT red. It is NOT yellow. It is small and grows in bunches. What is it?
hints: Cross out red and yellow fruits.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Two-circle Venn
problems.prompt: One circle = animals with 4 legs. Another = animals that swim. Where does a frog go? A dog? A fish? A bird?
problems.answer: frog: both; dog: 4 legs only; fish: swim only; bird: outside
problems.explain_prompt: Why does the frog land in the middle?

problems.title: Two doors
problems.prompt: Two doors. One says 'cake is here'. The other says 'cake is NOT here'. Exactly one sign is true. Which door has the cake?
hints: If door 1 is true, then cake is at 1, and door 2 ('not here') is false. Consistent!
If door 1 is false, cake is not at 1; door 2 says 'not here' = true. Cake at 2 — but door 2 means 'not at 2'? Re-check.
Best read: door 2 says cake is not at door 2. If true, cake at 1. So both readings give door 1.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Lost shoe
problems.prompt: Three kids each had two same-color shoes (red, blue, green). One shoe got lost. Now there are: 2 red, 1 blue, 2 green. What color is the lost shoe?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: One heavier coin
problems.prompt: You have 3 coins. One is heavier than the others. Using a balance once, can you find which? How?
problems.answer: put 1 vs 1; if equal, the third is heavier
hints: Try putting one coin on each side.
If they balance, the leftover is heavy.
If not, the heavy side has the heavy coin.
problems.explain_prompt: Why does one weighing work for 3 coins?

problems.title: Sock drawer in the dark
problems.prompt: A drawer has 3 red socks and 3 blue socks, all mixed up. In the dark, how many socks must you grab to be SURE you have a matching pair?
hints: Then your 3rd sock matches one of those.
Two colors → 3 grabs guarantees a pair.
problems.explain_prompt: What if there were 3 colors? How many socks then?
problems.parent_extension: Try with kids' actual sock drawer (eyes closed, with parent supervising).

problems.title: How many sides?
problems.prompt: Triangle: how many sides? Square? Hexagon (6 sides — bee's honeycomb shape)? Circle?
hints: Trace each shape with your finger.
Count corners — they match the sides.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Folding shapes
problems.prompt: Cut out a heart. Fold it down the middle. Do the halves match? That's symmetry. Now try with a square. With a triangle. Which folds work?
hints: Some triangles only fold one way.
problems.explain_prompt: What does a 'line of symmetry' do?
problems.parent_extension: Find symmetric things at home — leaves, faces, letters.

problems.title: Tangram square
problems.prompt: Use 2 small triangles to make a square. Use 2 small triangles to make a bigger triangle. What other shape can you make?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Position words
problems.prompt: Put a block ABOVE your pencil. BELOW it. To the LEFT. RIGHT. BETWEEN two pencils. Now describe where your shoe is in the room using these words.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Mental rotation
problems.prompt: Picture a capital letter L. Now rotate it a quarter-turn clockwise. What letter or shape does it look like? Try a quarter-turn the other way.
hints: Use your hand to mime the L.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Paper folding holes
problems.prompt: Fold a square in half, then in half again. Punch a small hole near the corner. Unfold. How many holes? Where are they?
problems.explain_prompt: What if you folded 3 times? How many holes?
problems.parent_extension: Predict before unfolding — that's the math.

problems.title: Cube faces
problems.prompt: A cube has 6 faces. How many edges? How many corners (vertices)? Try to count without missing any.
hints: Each face has 4 edges, but shared.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Around the rug
problems.prompt: A rectangular rug is 5 steps long and 3 steps wide. How many steps to walk all the way around it?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Cut the rectangle
problems.prompt: A rectangle is 4 squares long and 2 squares tall (8 squares total). Cut along grid lines into two pieces of 4 squares each. Find 3 different ways.
problems.explain_prompt: What do all the cuts have in common?

problems.title: Flat or solid
problems.prompt: Sort these into flat (2D) and solid (3D): square, ball, book, circle, can, triangle.
problems.answer: flat: square, circle, triangle; solid: ball, book, can
hints: Solid = you can hold space inside.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: How many pencils long?
problems.prompt: Use pencils end-to-end to measure the table. About how many pencils long is it? Now use crayons. Why is the number different?
problems.explain_prompt: What does it tell us when a smaller unit gives a bigger number?

problems.title: Half past three
problems.prompt: Where does the minute hand point at 'half past three'? Where does the hour hand point?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: 25 cents many ways
problems.prompt: Find three different coin combinations that make 25 cents. (Pennies = 1, nickels = 5, dimes = 10, quarter = 25.)
problems.parent_extension: Use real coins. Pay 25 cents 3 different ways.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Change from a dollar
problems.prompt: A toy costs 65 cents. You pay with 1 dollar (100 cents). How much change?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Heavier or lighter?
problems.prompt: Hold a book in one hand and a pencil in the other. Which is heavier? Which feels easier to hold? How do you know?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: How long did it take?
problems.prompt: Soccer practice started at 4:00. It ended at 4:45. How many minutes long? If we started at 4:00 and went 30 minutes, what time did we stop?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Walking feet
problems.prompt: Measure the length of a room in your-feet (heel-to-toe). Now have a parent measure the same room in their-feet. Why are the counts different?
problems.answer: parent's foot is longer → smaller count
hints: Need a STANDARD unit to share.
problems.explain_prompt: Why do people use feet/inches the same way?

problems.title: Cover with squares
problems.prompt: Cover a rectangle 3 squares wide and 2 squares tall with square tiles. How many tiles? Now 4 by 3.
problems.explain_prompt: How does this connect to multiplication?

problems.title: Warmer or cooler?
problems.prompt: It was 60 degrees this morning. Now it's 75. Did it get warmer or cooler? By how much?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Filling the bottle
problems.prompt: A bottle holds 8 cups. You pour in 3 cups. Then 2 more. How many more cups to fill it?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Outfit choices
problems.prompt: You have 2 shirts (red, blue) and 3 pants (black, gray, tan). How many different outfits?
problems.explain_prompt: Why does it equal 2 × 3?
problems.parent_extension: Use real clothes — count outfits for school.

problems.title: Ice cream cone
problems.prompt: Pick 1 cone (sugar or waffle) and 1 flavor (vanilla, chocolate, strawberry, mint). How many different cones?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Making 12
problems.prompt: How many ways to make 12 using two whole numbers? (Order: 3+9 same as 9+3.) List them.
problems.explain_prompt: Why stop at 6+6?

problems.title: Making 15 with three
problems.prompt: Three different whole numbers, all bigger than 0, that add to 15. How many sets?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Handshakes
problems.prompt: 3 friends each shake hands once with everyone else. How many handshakes total? What about 4 friends?
hints: Draw dots and lines between them.
problems.explain_prompt: If you add a 5th friend, how many new handshakes?

problems.title: Paths on a 2x2 grid
problems.prompt: Move from corner to opposite corner of a 2-by-2 grid, only going right or up. How many different paths?
hints: Each path is 4 moves: 2 right, 2 up.
List orderings: RRUU, RURU, RUUR, URRU, URUR, UURR.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Three coin flips
problems.prompt: Flip a coin 3 times. List all the possible sequences (like HHT, HTT...). How many?
problems.explain_prompt: How many would there be for 4 flips?

problems.title: Sandwich choices
problems.prompt: Bread (white, wheat) + filling (cheese, ham, tuna). How many sandwiches?
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Three friends in line
problems.prompt: Anna, Ben, Coco line up at the door. How many different orders are possible? List them.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Domino pairs
problems.prompt: Dominoes have two ends, each with 0 to 6 dots. How many different dominoes if no domino repeats? (Hint: a 3-5 is the same as 5-3.)
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Race to 20
problems.prompt: Two players. Take turns adding 1 or 2 to a running total. The first to say 20 wins. Play 3 times. Can you find a winning trick?
hints: If opponent says 18, you say 20. If they say 19, you say 20.
problems.explain_prompt: Why is 17 a magic number?
problems.parent_extension: Try Race to 21, then Race to 50 (add 1, 2, or 3).

problems.title: Pig — risk and reward
problems.prompt: Roll a die. Add to your turn-total. Roll again or HOLD (keep). If you roll 1, you lose this turn's total. First to 50 wins. What's your strategy?
hints: Rolling more risks losing it all.
problems.parent_extension: Play 5 rounds and track who wins. Discuss strategy after.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Target 7
problems.prompt: Roll 2 dice. Player chooses to add or subtract. Closest to 7 wins each round. First to 5 wins overall.
hints: If you roll 5 and 2: add → 7. Bullseye!
Pick add or subtract to land closest.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Guess my number
problems.prompt: One player thinks of a number 1–50. Other guesses; for each guess, hear 'higher' or 'lower'. Try to find it in 6 guesses or fewer.
hints: Always guess the middle of what's left.
Each guess cuts the range in half.
problems.explain_prompt: Why does guessing 25 first beat guessing 1?

problems.title: Race to 100
problems.prompt: Two players take turns adding 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10. First to 100 wins. Find the magic numbers.
hints: If you say 89, opponent says 90–99; you finish.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Connect the corners
problems.prompt: On a 3-by-3 grid of dots, players take turns drawing a line between adjacent dots (no diagonals). Whoever closes a square claims it. Most squares wins.
hints: Avoid drawing the third side of any box.
Force the other player to give you a square.
Long chains matter at the end.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: War with adding
problems.prompt: Each player flips 2 cards (Aces=1, faces=10). Add them. Highest sum wins all 4 cards. Most cards at the end wins.
problems.parent_extension: Try with 3 cards and add. Or subtract smaller from larger.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Number line jump
problems.prompt: Mark a number line 0 to 30. Roll a die: jump that many forward. Roll a coin: heads = forward, tails = back. First to 30 wins (must land exactly).
hints: Last few squares need exact rolls.
Going back can help if too high.
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?

problems.title: Take stones (Nim)
problems.prompt: 21 stones. Take turns removing 1, 2, or 3 stones. Whoever takes the LAST stone wins. Find the strategy.
hints: If 21 to start, you go first: take 1 → leave 20.
Mirror: opponent takes k, you take 4-k.
problems.explain_prompt: Why is 'leaving a multiple of 4' a winning move?

problems.title: Shut the box
problems.prompt: Numbers 1–9 are open. Roll 2 dice. Close numbers that sum to your roll (e.g., roll 7: close 7, OR 3+4, OR 1+6, OR 1+2+4). Goal: close them all. Score = unclosed sum (lower is better).
problems.explain_prompt: How do you know? Can you show it another way?
